_meta.method: Compiled from English Wikipedia entries on the Five Houses of Chan ancestral temples and major modern Chan training monasteries. Coordinates from Wikipedia infoboxes; flagged in notes when approximate.
_meta.coverageEstimate: Ancestral seats of the Linji, Caodong, and Yunmen schools plus the most-prominent modern Chan revival monasteries. Many smaller historic temples (especially in Sichuan, Fujian, Yunnan) and contemporary Chan-training monasteries omitted.
knownGaps: Many provincial Chan training monasteries (Sichuan, Fujian, Yunnan, Hunan)
Most Chan ancestor sites in poor preservation (Guishan, Yangshan)
Most temples primarily oriented to Pure Land or scholastic Buddhism even though historically Chan-affiliated

places.name: Linji Temple (Linji Yuan)
places.lineage: Chinese Chan (Linji — ancestral seat of the Linji house)
places.address: Linji Lu, Zhengding County, Shijiazhuang, Hebei
places.notes: Founding monastery of the Linji school of Chan, where Linji Yixuan taught in the 9th century. Restored in the 1980s and again under Master Jing Hui; the Chenglin Pagoda dates to the 9th century.

places.name: Bailin Chan Temple
places.address: Bailin Si, Zhao County, Shijiazhuang, Hebei 051530
places.notes: Where Zhaozhou Congshen taught for 40 years in the 9th century. Major modern Chan-revival training monastery rebuilt under Master Jing Hui (Jinghui) from 1988; flagship of the 'Living Chan' movement.

places.name: Zhenru Chan Temple (Yunju Shan)
places.address: Yunju Mountain, Yongxiu County, Jiujiang, Jiangxi
places.notes: Largest active Caodong training monastery in China; site of Daoying Yu's 9th-century Daoying Chansi and continuously a Caodong seat since. Rebuilt under Master Xuyun in the 1950s.

places.name: Tiantong Temple
places.lineage: Chinese Chan (Caodong — historic — where Dōgen received transmission)
places.notes: Major Caodong ancestral temple founded 300 CE. Where Tiantong Rujing taught Dōgen 1223–1227, transmitting the Sōtō line to Japan. Active Chan training monastery.

places.name: Asoka Temple (Ayuwang)
places.lineage: Chinese Chan (Linji — historic — where Eisai studied)
places.address: Ayuwang Lu, Beilun District, Ningbo, Zhejiang
places.notes: Founded 282 CE; one of the Five Mountain Chan temples of southern Song China. Where Myōan Eisai studied Linji Chan before establishing Rinzai in Japan.

places.name: Lingyin Temple
places.lineage: Chinese Chan (Linji — major historic Chan temple)
places.notes: Founded 328 CE; one of the wealthiest and largest Chan temples in China. Listed among the Five Mountains of southern Song Chan.

places.name: Jingci Temple
places.lineage: Chinese Chan (Linji — Five Mountains historic temple)
places.address: South of West Lake, Xihu District, Hangzhou
places.notes: Founded 954 CE; one of the original Five Mountains of Linji Chan in Southern Song China.

places.name: Jinshan Temple
places.address: Jinshan Park, Runzhou District, Zhenjiang, Jiangsu
places.notes: Founded 4th century; major Linji training monastery of late imperial China. Source of one of two main Chan training rule traditions ('Jinshan style').

places.name: Gaomin Temple
places.address: Gaomin Si, Hanjiang District, Yangzhou, Jiangsu
places.notes: Founded Sui dynasty; revived by Master Laiguo in the 20th century. One of the most rigorous active Chan training monasteries in China; Sheng Yen trained here.

places.name: Putuoshan Puji Temple
places.lineage: Chinese Chan (Linji — major historic mountain temple)
places.notes: Main monastery on Mount Putuo, one of the Four Sacred Mountains of Chinese Buddhism (Avalokiteśvara's bodhimanḍa). Historically Linji training site.

places.name: Xuedou Temple
places.address: Xuedou Mountain, Xikou Town, Fenghua District, Ningbo
places.notes: Where Xuedou Chongxian (980–1052) compiled the Hekiganroku (Blue Cliff Record) base verses. Yunmen-school heritage temple.

places.name: Daming Temple
places.notes: Founded 5th century; where Jianzhen (Ganjin) lived before founding Tōshōdai-ji in Japan. Active monastery.

places.name: Donglin Temple
places.notes: Founded 386 CE on Mount Lu; one of the most historically significant Chan-Pure Land temples in China.

places.name: Guoqing Temple
places.lineage: Chinese Chan (Tiantai school home temple — historic)
places.address: Tiantai Mountain, Tiantai County, Taizhou, Zhejiang
places.notes: Founded 598 CE; founding seat of the Tiantai school. Active monastery; Saichō transmitted Tiantai (Tendai) to Japan from here.

places.name: Hanshan Temple
places.address: Hanshansi Lu, Gusu District, Suzhou, Jiangsu
places.notes: Founded 6th century; named for Tang-era Chan poet Hanshan. Active Linji-affiliated monastery in Suzhou's old city.

places.name: Caotang Temple
places.notes: Founded 401 CE; where Kumarajiva translated the Lotus Sutra. Active Buddhist monastery; key proto-Chan textual heritage site.
